education.summary: Dissertation: Sensing Prehistory: A Framework Reconciling Scientific Modeling and Human Experience through Affordance Theory.
Thesis: Hydrological Landscape Analysis of a Sinuous Depression, Yaxnohcah, Mexico.

highlights: Managing spatial data and delivery implementation scenarios within the Ottawa region.
Providing technical GIS support for strategic route planning and logistics.
Conducted archaeological field surveys and technical assessments.
Curated an extensive EndNote database and assembled references under tight deadlines for journal publications.

work.company: University of New Brunswick Anthropology Lab
highlights: Coordinated and digitized decades-old records and field reports and cross-referenced these reports with artifact collections.
Created site reconstruction using CorelDRAW, modelling indigenous dwellings, artifact distributions, and temporal change.
Photographed and catalogued artifact collections. Prepared museum display.

work.company: University of New Brunswick Anthropology Lab
highlights: Curated archaeological collection, updating incomplete catalogue entries. Digitally restored and enhanced artifact and excavation photos using Adobe Photoshop.
Digitally rephotographed numerous artifacts and digitized paper archival records.
Digitized archival documents, performed quality control, and managed image database.

work.company: University of New Brunswick Anthropology Lab
highlights: Curated archaeological collection, extensively reorganizing precontact and historic artifact collections. Relocated artifacts to new storage containers.
Verified all artifacts were catalogued and identified incomplete entries.

Created an open and accepting course space that accommodated a variety of learning styles: Provided space for self-expression through directed introduction activities, space for blog-style biographies, and created space for all students to share and express ideas.
Held one-on-one meetings with every student to develop a connection and understand their individual needs in terms of feeling safe and learning.

Encouraged peer-based learning by dedicating time every session for group discussions and problem solving, assigning peer-reviewed projects, and creating online discussion boards to provide an additional venue for the exchange of ideas and collaborations.
Designed assignments and examinations that provided flexibility for students to articulate their ideas.
Favoured written answers over multiple choice as students are better able to communicate their understanding using this medium.

Assigned an ‘unessay’ rather than a traditional essay. This assignment allowed students to research a traditional essay topic but placed no restrictions on the medium and methods they used to communicate their research findings.
Provided extensive feedback on graded work and allowed students the option of oral rather than written examinations.
Administered lectures, graded course material, assisted in organizing sessions, held office hours and mentored students.
Helped adapt course material to Zoom-based learning following the onset of the COVID-19 pandemic.
Administered lectures, ran and oversaw all online non-lecture components of the course material, assisted in organizing classes, held office hours, and mentored students.
Administered lectures, graded course material, and assisted in organizing sessions.
Assisted in organizing sessions, creating, administering, and grading assignments and examinations, held office hours and mentored students.

projects.name: Poverty Point Research Project (Louisiana, USA)
highlights: Directed a small team of researchers. Integrated GIS and phenomenology to examine prehistoric landscape use by complex hunter-gatherers, with an emphasis on hydrological modelling and spatial analysis.
Established connections with local authorities and the community. Conducted drone and ground survey of the site and created a photogrammetry model using the imagery.
Developed, planned, and performed a project combining experience-based learning with extensive GIS analysis.

projects.name: Proyecto Arqueológico Yaxnohcah (Yaxnohcah, Mexico)
highlights: Developed a GIS and archaeological research project to explore the agricultural canal and directed a small team, working closely with the local and Indigenous community.
Conducted extensive GIS-based hydrological analyses and field excavations. Applied for and received funding for the project.
Created high-quality maps, visualizations, and reports, and communicated findings at conferences and publications (thesis and reports).
